summary: Integration smoke testing of Mission Control v8 ↔ Baseline OS deployment setup. All 8 review-request scenarios verified. 12/12 pytest cases pass; browser login flow proven end-to-end (admin → /app dashboard, no CSRF mismatch). Backend proxy, API-key gate, runtime handshake, sync doctor + push, public marketing pages, and unauthenticated 401 all green.

minor.issue: Runtime health flips to 'red' between heartbeats (heartbeat interval is 60s, threshold appears <60s). After a fresh `bun run scripts/mc.ts sync push` both runtimes report health=green; ~60s later they may show red until the watch loop fires again. Not blocking but the review request expected steady green — consider tightening heartbeat interval to 30s or relaxing the green threshold to ≥90s.

CSP violation in console: 'Executing inline script violates ... script-src self nonce-... strict-dynamic ...'. Coming from the Google Sign-In bootstrap. Login still works via username/password; cosmetic but worth fixing the nonce propagation on the GIS script tag.

action_items: (Optional) Tighten Baseline OS heartbeat cadence or relax the runtime 'green' staleness threshold so the handshake doesn't flip red between 60s pings.
(Optional) Add nonce to the Google Identity Services inline bootstrap so CSP doesn't warn on /login.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend shim /app/backend/server.py reverse-proxy correctly preserves Host header for CSRF — login from external preview URL works as advertised.
API key gate in /app/src/proxy.ts accepts x-api-key for /api/runtime/handshake AND /api/agents (validated both 200 with key, 401 without).
Baseline OS sync CLI (/app/baseline-os/scripts/mc.ts) is the right shape: doctor returns structured JSON with ok-per-check; push returns pushed/failed/queued. Both consumed cleanly by pytest.
Standalone Next.js process is correctly launched via /app/.node22/bin/node /app/.next/standalone/server.js — supervisor logs show clean startup, migrations applied, scheduler running.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded admin + the two Baseline OS runtimes already registered (claude-code@..., hermes@..., workspace_id=1).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Integration suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_mc_baseline_integration.py — runs in ~2s and covers login, auth gates, x-api-key, public pages, baseline console (internal :4173), and the bun sync CLI doctor+push. Use REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env. If the Baseline OS watch loop dies, the handshake will return runtimes with health=red — fix is to relaunch via `cd /app/baseline-os && PATH=/app/.bun/bin:$PATH bun run scripts/mc.ts sync push`.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues found. The previously reported CSRF origin mismatch (review-request item #7) is resolved — verified by both curl POST /api/auth/login (HTTP 200) and a real browser submission landing on /app. The fix in /app/backend/server.py preserving the original Host header is doing its job.
